video:video.video:title: Hong Kong court hears final arguments in former Tiananmen vigil organizers' national security trial
video:video.video:description: Chow Hang-tung and Lee Cheuk-yan, two former leaders of the Hong Kong Alliance in Support of Patriotic Democratic Movements of China, have pleaded not guilty.

video:video.video:title: Halsey is turning her attention to film
video:video.video:description: Grammy-nominated artist Halsey explains why she's at the Cannes Film Festival, including to network for her freshly announced horror, "Replacer." (May 18)

video:video.video:title: 'Pillion' star Harry Melling is ready to be transported back to Hogwarts
video:video.video:description: Harry Melling, who played Dudley Dursley in the original films, explains why he's looking forward to watching the new "Harry Potter" series. (May 18)

video:video.video:title: Judge allows gun and notebook as evidence at Mangione’s trial in UnitedHealthcare CEO’s killing
video:video.video:description: The judge allowed evidence obtained during a subsequent inventory of his backpack at the police station, but said evidence found during an initial search of Mangione’s backpack during his arrest at a McDonald’s restaurant must be suppressed.

video:video.video:title: Residents of Bulgaria capital are pleased following Dara's Eurovision victory and grand welcome home
video:video.video:description: Residents of Sofia reacted with excitement after Bulgarian singer, Dara, won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Workers form picket lines as strike shuts down the largest US commuter rail system
video:video.video:description: The strike shutting down North America’s largest commuter rail system will continue into a third day.

video:video.video:title: 'Extraordinary Attorney Woo' star Park Eun-bin shares career secrets
video:video.video:description: South Korean actor Park Eun-bin explains what has kept her going through 30 years in the industry, from child model to her latest role in "The WONDERfools." (May 18)

video:video.video:title: Argentina's southernmost outpost of Ushuaia fears hantavirus speculation will chill tourism
video:video.video:description: The Associated Press' Isabel Debre takes a look at how the Argentine wind-lashed city of Ushuaia that bills itself as the “end of the world” now fears for its future after it found itself at the center of speculation about the source of a deadly hantavirus outbreak.

video:video.video:title: Two Navy jets collide during Idaho air show
video:video.video:description: An air show in Idaho was called off when two Navy jets collided and crashed. The four crew members from both jets safely ejected and the crash is under investigation.

video:video.video:title: Protesters light bonfires during public transport strike in Kenya over fuel prices
video:video.video:description: Protests have erupted in Nairobi as a nationwide public transport strike begins over rising fuel prices. (AP video shot by Andrew Kasuku)

video:video.video:title: Police on Long Island deploy pepper spray on crowd lining up for high demand luxury watch
video:video.video:description: A scuffle erupted between deputies and watch enthusiasts who had camped outside a New York store on Saturday, prompting officers to deploy pepper spray on the crowd.

video:video.video:title: A tour of la Croisette
video:video.video:description: AP takes you on a tour of Boulevard de la Croisette - the beating heart of the annual Cannes Film Festival.

video:video.video:title: Long Island Rail Road shuts down as workers strike
video:video.video:description: A marathon day of negotiations that finished early Monday failed to end a strike that shut down the Long Island Rail Road, North America’s largest commuter rail system, setting the stage for a rough start to the work week for about 250,000 commuters.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 18
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, May 18th: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship docks in Rotterdam; Luigi Mangione in court for evidence hearing; Turkey's Erdogan denounces Israeli navy's interception of Gaza-bound flotilla; Epstein files exhibition opens in New York City.

video:video.video:title: Two suspects killed in shooting at a San Diego mosque
video:video.video:description: The two suspects in a Monday shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego are dead, according to a police source.

video:video.video:title: Alex Murdaugh sues clerk whose behavior led state Supreme Court to overturn his murder convictions
video:video.video:description: The legal defense team for Alex Murdaugh announced a lawsuit against the court clerk whose behavior during his murder trial led the South Carolina Supreme Court to overturn his convictions and life sentence for the deaths of his wife and son.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship docks in the Netherlands for disinfection as final crew disembark
video:video.video:description: A cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak docked at the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection Monday, wrapping up a troubled journey that put international health authorities on alert.

video:video.video:title: White House official says there are 'no cases of Ebola in America'
video:video.video:description: At a White House event about prescription drugs with President Donald Trump, Heidi Overton, a medical doctor who is deputy director of the White House Domestic Policy Council, was on hand to address any questions from reporters about the Ebola infections.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: Brazilian artisan crafts replica World Cup trophies to the joy of locals and celebrities alike
video:video.video:description: Brazilian soccer fan and former metallurgist Jarbas Meneghini Carlini has been combining his love for the sport with his business, making replicas of the FIFA World Cup trophy in his workshop in western Rio de Janeiro.

video:video.video:title: Robert Downey Jr. says live theater is 'vital'
video:video.video:description: Robert Downey, Jr. says live theater is "vital" as he and his wife, producer Susan Downey, are honored at Lincoln Center Theater's annual gala. (May 18)

video:video.video:title: 'I consider myself lucky': Survivor recounts Modena car attack
video:video.video:description: A man injured in a car-ramming and stabbing attack in Italy’s northern city of Modena said on Monday he considered himself lucky not to have been more seriously injured, and thanked those who came to his aid.

video:video.video:title: Ebola outbreak designated global health emergency by WHO with Congo to open three treatment centers
video:video.video:description: The Democratic Republic of Congo will open three Ebola treatment centers in the eastern province of Ituri after an outbreak of a rare strain of the virus that has no approved vaccines or treatments, as international health officials warned of a growing risk of regional spread.

video:video.video:title: Armed members of Iran's nomadic Bakhtiari tribe march in Tehran amid tensions with US
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of members of Iran's Bakhtiari tribe gathered in Tehran on Monday to pledge allegiance to Iran's supreme leader. (AP Video by Mohsen Ganji)

video:video.video:title: Protests in Venezuela after elderly mother dies days after learning missing son died in prison
video:video.video:description: Protesters demonstrated Monday in Venezuela’s capital in memory of a woman who died over the weekend, just days after learning that her son had died in state custody 10 months ago.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship docks in the Netherlands
video:video.video:description: The cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak has begun entering the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection, wrapping up a troubled journey that put international health authorities on alert.

video:video.video:title: Long Island Rail Road shuts down as workers strike
video:video.video:description: A marathon day of negotiations that finished early Monday failed to end a strike that shut down the Long Island Rail Road, North America’s largest commuter rail system, setting the stage for a rough start to the work week for about 250,000 commuters.

video:video.video:title: Commuters diverted to subways as LIRR strike disrupts millions
video:video.video:description: Workers got up extra early Monday to navigate a maze of car, bus and subway routes after a strike shut down the Long Island Railroad in New York, North America’s largest commuter rail system.

video:video.video:title: Floral festival takes over wealthy London neighbourhood
video:video.video:description: Shops and public spaces in London's affluent neighborhood are decorated in blooms for an annual celebration that runs parallel to the world-famous Chelsea Flower Show.

video:video.video:title: Ship with aid from Mexico and Uruguay sails into Havana port
video:video.video:description: A ship carrying humanitarian aid from Mexico and Uruguay arrived in Havana Monday as Cuba faces worsening shortages of food, fuel and electricity.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy claims a 'significant' number of Russian regions are in a state of bankruptcy
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy on Monday claimed a "significant number" of Russian regions were in a state of bankruptcy.

video:video.video:title: Professional mourners mix tribal tradition with Kenya’s widespread Christianity
video:video.video:description: Loud and expressive mourners are common at funerals around the world—but in parts of Kenya, some of them may be paid workers.

video:video.video:title: King Charles and Queen Camilla attend annual Chelsea Flower Show in London
video:video.video:description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla visited London’s renowned Chelsea Flower Show Monday.

video:video.video:title: A third 'Sherlock' film for Robert Downey Jr?
video:video.video:description: Robert Downey Jr. and his producer wife Susan Downey aren't ready to confirm that a third "Sherlock Holmes" film is in the works, calling it "the greatest mystery."

video:video.video:title: Nearby resident recounts San Diego mosque shooting
video:video.video:description: Two teenage gunmen opened fire at an Islamic Center in San Diego on Monday and killed three men before killing themselves a few blocks away, police said.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he put off 'very major attack' on Iran Tuesday after 'big discussions' with Tehran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said he is postponing a "major" planned military strike on Iran Tuesday at the request of Middle East allies because “serious negotiations” are underway, saying he hopes the delay lasts “for a little while, hopefully, maybe forever."

video:video.video:title: Shooting at San Diego mosque kills 3 men and both suspects are dead, police say
video:video.video:description: A shooting at a San Diego mosque on Monday killed three men, and two teenage suspects are also dead, the police chief said.

video:video.video:title: How do global events affect gas prices at the pump?
video:video.video:description: Gas prices move up and down in response to disruptions in supply and spikes in demand. Many global events such as conflicts, sanctions or decisions by major oil producing countries can limit or create uncertainty about the supply of oil.

video:video.video:title: Police: Two suspects kill 3 people at a San Diego mosque before killing themselves
video:video.video:description: Authorities say two teenage suspects opened fire at an Islamic center in San Diego, California, killing three men before taking their own lives.

video:video.video:title: San Diego police say they are responding to an active shooter at a local Islamic Center
video:video.video:description: San Diego police are responding to an active shooter at the Islamic Center of San Diego. Officer Anthony Carrasco says they believe people have been shot.

video:video.video:title: Interfaith march in Jerusalem's Old City seeks to provide show of unity
video:video.video:description: Dozens of activists participated in an interfaith march through Jerusalem on Monday. (AP Video by Shlomo Mor)

video:video.video:title: Nicholas Galitzine says playing He-Man was something he had to 'earn'
video:video.video:description: At the premiere of “Masters of the Universe,” Nicholas Galitzine said playing He-Man was a role he felt he had to “earn.” The film hits theaters June 5. (May 18)

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 18
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, May 18th: Trump and Iran send new warnings; Ukrainian drone strikes near Moscow; South Carolina to take up new redistricting measure; New York’s Long Island Railroad on strike.

video:video.video:title: Adam Driver responds to Lena Dunham
video:video.video:description: Actor Adam Driver kept tight lipped when asked if he had a response for Lena Dunham after comments made about him by her in her book. (May 18)

video:video.video:title: Israeli troops intercept vessels from a flotilla trying to breach the blockade of Gaza
video:video.video:description: More than 50 vessels departed from the port in Marmaris, Turkey, last week in what the organizers of the Global Sumud Flotilla described as the final leg of their planned journey to Gaza’s shores.

video:video.video:title: Iran soccer team departs for Turkey training camp ahead of World Cup
video:video.video:description: Iran's semi-official Tasnim news agency posted a video showing members of the country's soccer team signing Jerseys ahead of their trip.

video:video.video:title: Adam Driver responds to Lena Dunham
video:video.video:description: Actor Adam Driver kept tight lipped when asked if he had a response for Lena Dunham after comments made about him by her in her book.

video:video.video:title: Cate Blanchett: small acts are the key to freedom
video:video.video:description: Cate Blanchett calls for engagement in society and the rejection of fear-based narratives, as her Displacement Film Fund announces its 2026 cohort of recipients, in Cannes.

video:video.video:title: Trump holding off on Iran military strike as 'serious negotiations' underway
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said he is holding off on a military strike on Iran planned for Tuesday because “serious negotiations” are underway to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Russia's Putin to visit China days after Trump met with President Xi
video:video.video:description: Russia's President Vladimir Putin will visit China Tuesday and Wednesday this week, the Chinese Foreign Minister announced.

video:video.video:title: Village festival combines catching fish with clearing weeds and silt
video:video.video:description: Villagers wade through a spring covered with algae, clearing the weeds. It's a century old tradition in the Himalayan region of Indian-controlled Kashmir that combines environmental conservation with a community celebration.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief Tedros 'deeply concerned about the scale and speed' of Ebola outbreak
video:video.video:description: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak a public health emergency of international concern on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Kylie likes to turn everything off
video:video.video:description: Pop icon Kylie Minogue explains why you won't see much self-shot phone footage in new Netflix documentary "KYLIE."

video:video.video:title: 1.5°C: what it means and why it matters
video:video.video:description: Scientists and the UN are acknowledging the Paris climate treaty goal of limiting warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius is now implausible without it getting warmer and then being artificially cooled.

video:video.video:title: Middle Age-inspired sand sculptures at Danish festival
video:video.video:description: A host of international sand sculptors are headed to the small Danish seaside town of Hundested for its fifteenth annual sand sculpture festival.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief says he is 'deeply concerned about the scale and speed' of Ebola outbreak
video:video.video:description: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak a public health emergency of international concern on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: Negotiators reach a deal to end strike on North America’s busiest commuter rail system
video:video.video:description: Negotiators have reached a deal to end a strike that has brought North America’s largest commuter rail system to a standstill.

video:video.video:title: Putin to visit China days after Trump met with President Xi
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is traveling to China to meet with Chinese leader Xi Jinping less than a week after U.S.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 19
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, May 19th: Trump says he called off strike on Iran; 3 men and two teenage suspects dead after San Diego mosque shooting; Nearly 120 dead from Ebola virus in Africa; Negotiators reach deal to end Long Island Rail Road strike.

video:video.video:title: Federal court rejects Elon Musk's claims against OpenAI, saying he filed his lawsuit too late
video:video.video:description: A federal court on Monday dismissed claims filed against OpenAI and its top executives by Elon Musk, who accused them of betraying a shared vision for it to remain a nonprofit dedicated to guiding artificial intelligence’s development for the good of humanity.

video:video.video:title: Russia-China ties at 'unprecedented level,' Putin says ahead of China visit
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said Russia-China ties have reached a “truly unprecedented level” in a video address released by the Kremlin on Tuesday, ahead of his visit to China for talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: Croak monsieur! Australian shopper finds live frog in grocery store salad
video:video.video:description: When Australian farmer Rhys Smoker announced he’d found a live frog in a bag of lettuce, his housemates didn’t believe him.

video:video.video:title: Fast-growing brush fire in Southern California threatens homes
video:video.video:description: The fire was reported Monday just after 10 a.m. in Simi Valley, a city in Ventura County northwest of Los Angeles.

video:video.video:title: Former LAPD detective Mark Fuhrman, convicted of lying at OJ Simpson trial, dies
video:video.video:description: Former Los Angeles police detective Mark Fuhrman, who was convicted of lying during testimony at the O.J. Simpson murder trial, has died. He was 74.

video:video.video:title: "Masters of the Universe" arrives in Hollywood
video:video.video:description: Nicholas Galitzine, Camila Mendes, Idris Elba attend the premiere of the highly anticipated fantasy reboot "Masters of the Universe" at the TCL Chinese Theatre in Hollywood.

video:video.video:title: Kylie Minogue's emotional documentary ride
video:video.video:description: Australian pop icon Kylie Minogue discusses the making of Netflix's three-part documentary "KYLIE" on her life - examining her highs, lows and everything in between.

video:video.video:title: Billie Jean King, 82, earns a college degree 65 years after starting at Cal State LA
video:video.video:description: Billie Jean King celebrated earning a college degree 65 years after first taking classes. The 82-year-old sports icon has received her bachelor's in history from Cal State Los Angeles.

video:video.video:title: Rep. Raskin calls Trump's $1.7 billion IRS settlement his latest 'slush fund'
video:video.video:description: The lead Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee is calling the Trump administration's creation of a $1.7 billion fund to compensate allies of the Republican president who believe they were mistreated by the Biden-era Justice Department a "slush fund."

video:video.video:title: 1.5°C: what it means and why it matters
video:video.video:description: Scientists and the UN are acknowledging the Paris climate treaty goal of limiting warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius warming is now implausible without it getting warmer and then being artificially cooled.

video:video.video:title: A de-extinction company has hatched live chicks from an artificial eggshell
video:video.video:description: Colossal Biosciences says 26 baby chickens were born from a 3D printed lattice structure that mimics an eggshell.

video:video.video:title: People gather at San Diego mosque a day after deadly shooting
video:video.video:description: People embrace as police stand outside the Islamic Center of San Diego where three men were killed in an attack on Monday, including a security guard whose actions they deemed heroic.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he was 'an hour away' from ordering strikes on Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday he was “an hour away” from ordering strikes on Iran before Gulf allies urged him to pause, saying they were close to a deal.

video:video.video:title: New deal is fair for employees and does not raise prices for commuters, LIRR president says
video:video.video:description: New deal is fair for employees and does not raise prices for commuters, LIRR president says

video:video.video:title: Democratic leaders in Mississippi mobilize against redistricting efforts
video:video.video:description: Mississippi Democratic leaders evoked the Civil Rights Movement as they urged citizens to get involved in the fight against redistricting efforts that could reshape electoral power in the state.

video:video.video:title: Blanche faces questions from lawmakers over a fund to compensate Trump allies
video:video.video:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche defended the creation of a $1.776 billion fund to compensate individuals who believe they were targeted politically.

video:video.video:title: Uganda puts more than 100 people in quarantine while Congo sets up Ebola treatment centers
video:video.video:description: The Ugandan government has confirmed that over 100 people are being quarantined at an undisclosed location as they are monitored for signs and symptoms of a rare type of Ebola virus.

video:video.video:title: Pennsylvania voters cast primary ballots, setting stage for the fall election
video:video.video:description: Voters in a bellwether county in suburban Philadelphia cast their primary ballots, setting the stage for November's election. (AP Video: Tassanee Vejpongsa)

video:video.video:title: Lawyers for the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk ask to seal evidence and parts of a key hearing
video:video.video:description: Lawyers for the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk make their case to bar reporters and the public from parts of a key upcoming hearing and seal some evidence after the judge rejected their request to ban news cameras from the courtroom.

video:video.video:title: 'I Love Boosters' cast on how the film has influenced their consumer habits
video:video.video:description: Here's how making "I Love Boosters" changed Keke Palmer and her co-stars' consumer fashion choices.

video:video.video:title: Nicolas Winding Refn breaks down in Cannes
video:video.video:description: Director Nicolas Winding Refn broke down and cried during a press conference in Cannes while talking about his near-death experience.

video:video.video:title: Finnish divers recover two of the dead Italians from an underwater cave in the Maldives
video:video.video:description: Divers have recovered the bodies of two Italian divers from an underwater cave in the Maldives. The recovery took place Tuesday in an area that a government spokesperson says has challenging conditions with poor visibility and strong currents.

video:video.video:title: Myth vs. Market: Why 'Sell in May' Is outdated advice in 2026
video:video.video:description: 'Sell in May and Go Away' is a long-standing Wall Street strategy that suggests investors should sell stocks and avoid the market during the typically sluggish summer months.

video:video.video:title: Billie Jean King, 82, earns a college degree 65 years after starting at Cal State LA
video:video.video:description: Billie Jean King celebrated earning a college degree 65 years after first taking classes. The 82-year-old sports icon received her bachelor's in history from Cal State Los Angeles after walking across the stage with about 6,000 others in the Class of 2026.

video:video.video:title: North Korea's Naegohyang Women’s FC and South Korea's Suwon FC train for AFC Champions League match
video:video.video:description: North Korea's Naegohyang Women's FC and its South Korean opponent Suwon FC Women spoke ahead of the women's football club competition in Asia.

video:video.video:title: A third 'Sherlock' film for Robert Downey Jr?
video:video.video:description: Are Robert Downey Jr and his wife, "Sherlock" producer Susan Downey, ready to talk about the franchise's third film?

video:video.video:title: Long Island Rail Road to resume operations as deal reached to end strike
video:video.video:description: Trains are set to start rolling again on the Long Island Rail Road after a deal was reached to end a strike that had shut down the busiest commuter rail system in the country.

video:video.video:title: Japan and South Korea to boost cooperation on stable energy supply
video:video.video:description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi and South Korean President Lee Jae Myung have emphasized the need for cooperation amid global challenges, including the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Senators debate if Jan. 6 rioters get payouts from $1.8 billion fund tied to Trump's IRS lawsuit
video:video.video:description: U.S. Senators are debating whether people who carried out violence during the Jan. 6, 2021 riot at the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Cade Klubnik excited for opportunity to learn and grow as he begins NFL career
video:video.video:description: Cade Klubnik excited for opportunity to learn and grow as he begins NFL career

video:video.video:title: Official says San Diego mosque security guard 'delayed' shooters, saving lives
video:video.video:description: San Diego Police Chief Scott Wahl said the security guard of the Islamic Center of San Diego "delayed and distracted" the shooting suspects, which saved lives without a doubt.

video:video.video:title: Trump endorses Ken Paxton in Texas GOP primary, boosting his challenge to incumbent Sen. Cornyn
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday endorsed Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton in the GOP primary for U.S. Senate, supercharging his effort to oust incumbent Sen.

video:video.video:title: Bolivia protesters allied with ex-leader Morales march on capital as unrest widens
video:video.video:description: Followers of Bolivia’s influential ex-President Evo Morales on Monday joined a protest movement fueled by the nation’s worst economic crisis in a generation.

video:video.video:title: Putin visits China to reaffirm Russia ties as Xi also seeks stable US relations after Trump summit
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Tuesday night arrived in China to meet with Chinese leader Xi Jinping, less than a week after U.S.

video:video.video:title: Trump gives reporters a tour of his ballroom construction site
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump showed reporters the White House ballroom construction site Tuesday, defending the project and touting its security features after $1B in security funding for the project was struck down.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians report settlers torching agricultural lands on outskirts of central West Bank village
video:video.video:description: Residents of Al-Mughayyir, a village in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, said settlers had set fires to their agricultural land the night before and fired flares along the western edge of the village.

video:video.video:title: On World Bee Day: Honey production is a family affair for these beekeepers in Romania
video:video.video:description: Bees are an essential part of our food chain, with more than three quarters of global crops depending on pollinators.

video:video.video:title: New York federal judge bans ICE arrests at Manhattan immigration courts
video:video.video:description: Federal agents can no longer make arrests without exceptional circumstances in and around three Manhattan buildings where immigration proceedings occur, a judge ruled Monday.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the Ebola outbreak in Congo as WHO raises concerns
video:video.video:description: Health officials are raising alarm over a rare Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo that has caused more than 500 suspected cases and at least 134 suspected deaths, prompting the World Health Organization to declare a public health emergency.

video:video.video:title: GOP candidate for Ohio governor Vivek Ramaswamy announces proposal to curb medicaid fraud
video:video.video:description: At a press conference in Columbus, Vivek Ramaswamy, a Republican candidate for Ohio governor, declared that fighting Medicaid fraud will be his “absolute top priority” as governor, if elected.

video:video.video:title: Shopper finds a live frog in a grocery store salad in Australia
video:video.video:description: When Australian farmer Rhys Smoker announced he’d found a live frog in a bag of lettuce, his housemates didn’t believe him.

video:video.video:title: Khalid Alexander speaks about his friend, beloved San Diego mosque security guard killed on the job
video:video.video:description: One of the three people killed by two teen shooters at a San Diego mosque was a beloved security guard who acted quickly to prevent more deaths. (AP video by Ty ONeil)

video:video.video:title: Trump blasts Kentucky's Rep. Massie and teases endorsement in Texas race
video:video.video:description: Massie has been a thorn in the president’s side for pushing for the release of the Jeffrey Epstein files, opposing the war with Iran and voting against Trump’s signature tax legislation last year.

video:video.video:title: Trump shows reporters ballroom construction site as lawmakers balk at $1B for security
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is defending the White House ballroom he's building, showing off the construction site to a group of reporters on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Argentine investigators in Ushuaia search for source of deadly hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: Argentine investigators, searching for the source of a deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise that set out from the country last month, were trapping rodents in the forests surrounding the southernmost city of Ushuaia on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Commuters welcome return of Long Island train service as deal reached to end strike
video:video.video:description: Trains are rolling again on the Long Island Rail Road after a deal ended a strike that shut down the nation’s busiest commuter rail system.

video:video.video:title: Son of Mango fashion chain founder posts bail after arrest over father’s fatal fall
video:video.video:description: Jonathan Andic posted bail of 1 million euros ($1.15 million) on Tuesday following his arrest for alleged homicide in connection with a renewed investigation into the death of his father, Isak, the billionaire founder of the Spanish fashion brand.

video:video.video:title: Funeral in Venezuela for elderly mother of dead prisoner resonates with chants for justice
video:video.video:description: Emotions ran high at the funeral for Carmen Teresa Navas in Venezuela on Tuesday. The 82-year-old mother died days after learning that her son, Víctor Hugo Quero, had died in state custody.

video:video.video:title: Clashes erupt in Bolivia as protesters allied with ex-leader Morales march on capital
video:video.video:description: Followers of Bolivia’s influential ex-President Evo Morales on Monday joined a massive protest movement fueled by the nation’s worst economic crisis in a generation.

video:video.video:title: Bolivia’s capital under siege as protests and blockades deepen crisis for President Paz
video:video.video:description: La Paz has been cut off after nearly three weeks of blockades by protestors demanding the resignation of President Rodrigo Paz in the face of the economic crisis gripping the Andean nation.

video:video.video:title: Ed Gallrein defeats Thomas Massie in Kentucky, furthering Trump’s hold on GOP
video:video.video:description: Kentucky U.S. Rep. Thomas Massie lost his Republican House primary Tuesday in another test of President Donald Trump’s power over his party after he handpicked challenger and ultimate winner Ed Gallrein to oust the incumbent.

video:video.video:title: Israeli minister orders West Bank hamlet evicted after hearing he may face arrest warrant overseas
video:video.video:description: Israel's finance minister ordered the eviction of a Palestinian village in the Israeli-occupied West Bank that has long been a target of Israeli authorities on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Xi welcomes Putin for talks on economic cooperation and key international and regional issues
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin was welcomed by Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing Wednesday. Putin received a welcome by the honor guard, as well as young children waving Chinese and Russian flags.

video:video.video:title: Kansas City small businesses whipping up World Cup flavors and merch for arriving fans this summer
video:video.video:description: Local businesses around Kansas City are releasing twists to their signature products and other merchandise to join in on the World Cup festivities.

video:video.video:title: M23 rebels in Goma play down Ebola fears as WHO delegation visits treatment center and lab
video:video.video:description: A M23 rebel group spokesperson downplayed concerns about the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo as a team from the World Health Organisation visited a treatment centre in Nyiragongo Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Activist comments as Israeli forces intercept 6 more flotilla vessels headed for Gaza
video:video.video:description: In footage released by the Global Sumud Flotilla, Israeli forces on a vessel were seen intercepting activists at sea.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike on southern Gaza damages tents and water pipeline
video:video.video:description: Several tents sheltering displaced families and a water pipeline were damaged by an Israeli airstrike in Khan Younis, residents said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Putin sits down for talks with Xi in Beijing, praises bilateral cooperation
video:video.video:description: Russia's President Vladimir Putin held a meeting with China's leader Xi Jinping in Beijing on Wednesday meant to reaffirm ties.

video:video.video:title: North London celebrates as Arsenal wins its first Premier League title in 22 years
video:video.video:description: Celebrations continued into the night in north London on Tuesday after the Arsenal soccer team was crowned Premier League champion.

video:video.video:title: Dozens injured, buildings damaged as 5.8 magnitude earthquake strikes Peru's Pacific region
video:video.video:description: At least 27 people were injured after a 5.8-magnitude earthquake struck the Pacific region of Peru. No deaths were reported.
